exercises.translation: I went to see a movie.

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't connect the purpose of going with the verb 行く.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't express the purpose of going to do something.
wrong_choice_explanations.から: から means 'from' or 'because' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of going.

exercises.example_sentence: 友だちが遊び__来ました。

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't express the purpose of coming.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of the friend's visit.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't express the purpose of coming to do something.

exercises.example_sentence: 今度、日本語を勉強し__行こうと思います。
exercises.translation: I'm thinking of going to study Japanese next time.

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — 日本語を is already using を, and it doesn't connect purpose with going.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't express the purpose of going.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of going to do something.

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't express the purpose of going.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't connect the purpose with the movement verb.
wrong_choice_explanations.へ: へ indicates direction — but it doesn't express going for the purpose of doing something.

exercises.example_sentence: 母が手伝い__来てくれました。
exercises.translation: My mother came to help me.

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't express the purpose of coming.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't express the purpose of coming to do something.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of the mother's visit.

exercises.example_sentence: 海で泳ぎ__行きませんか。
exercises.translation: Won't you go swimming in the ocean?

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't connect the purpose of going with the verb.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で is already used for location (海で) — it doesn't express purpose of going.
wrong_choice_explanations.へ: へ indicates direction — but it doesn't express going for the purpose of doing something.

exercises.example_sentence: 病院へ検査を受け__行かなければなりません。
exercises.translation: I have to go to the hospital to get an examination.

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を is already used with 検査を — it doesn't connect purpose with the movement verb.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't express the purpose of going.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of going to do something.

exercises.example_sentence: 先生が教え__来てくださいました。
exercises.translation: The teacher came to teach us.

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を marks direct objects — it doesn't express the purpose of coming.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't express the purpose of coming to do something.
wrong_choice_explanations.から: から means 'from' or 'because' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of the teacher's visit.

exercises.example_sentence: 昼ご飯を食べ__行きましょう。

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を is already used with 昼ご飯を — it doesn't connect the purpose with going.
wrong_choice_explanations.で: で indicates location or means — it doesn't express the purpose of going.
wrong_choice_explanations.へ: へ indicates direction — but it doesn't express going for the purpose of doing something.

exercises.example_sentence: 図書館で本を読み__来ます。
exercises.translation: I'm going to the library to read books.

wrong_choice_explanations.を: を is already used with 本を — it doesn't express the purpose of going.
wrong_choice_explanations.と: と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of going to do something.
wrong_choice_explanations.から: から means 'from' or 'because' — it doesn't indicate the purpose of going.
